questions.q: Which numbered President of India was K.R. Narayanan?
questions.explanation: Kocheril Raman Narayanan was the tenth President of India.

questions.q: In which year did K.R. Narayanan begin his term as President of India?
questions.explanation: He assumed office as President in 1997.

questions.q: In which year did K.R. Narayanan complete his term as President of India?
questions.explanation: His presidential term concluded in 2002.

questions.q: K.R. Narayanan holds the distinction of being the first member of which community to become President of India?
questions.explanation: He was the first Dalit to hold the office of President.

questions.q: Before entering electoral politics, K.R. Narayanan had a distinguished career in which field?
questions.explanation: He had earlier been a distinguished diplomat.

questions.q: What does the 'K.R.' in K.R. Narayanan stand for?
questions.explanation: His full name was Kocheril Raman Narayanan.

questions.q: Which office did K.R. Narayanan hold immediately before becoming President?
questions.explanation: He served as Vice-President of India before being elected President.

questions.q: The President's office held by Narayanan is the constitutional head of what?
questions.explanation: The President of India is the constitutional head of state.

questions.q: Roughly how many years did K.R. Narayanan serve as President?
questions.explanation: He served from 1997 to 2002, a term of about five years.

questions.q: K.R. Narayanan's presidential term began closest to which milestone year for India?
questions.explanation: He took office in 1997, the fiftieth year of India's independence.

questions.q: As Vice-President before his presidency, Narayanan served as ex-officio Chairman of which House?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: The President, the office Narayanan held, is elected by an electoral college of which members?
options: Elected MPs and members of state legislative assemblies
questions.explanation: The President is elected by an electoral college of elected MPs and members of state legislative assemblies.

questions.q: As President, K.R. Narayanan was the supreme commander of which body?
questions.explanation: The President of India is the supreme commander of the armed forces.

questions.q: Narayanan's presidency immediately followed that of which President?
questions.explanation: He succeeded Shankar Dayal Sharma, the ninth President.

questions.q: Narayanan's presidency was immediately followed by that of which President?
questions.explanation: A.P.J. Abdul Kalam, the eleventh President, succeeded Narayanan.

questions.q: The normal term of office of the President, which Narayanan held, is how many years?
questions.explanation: The President is elected for a term of five years.

questions.q: Which sequence correctly orders Narayanan's two highest constitutional offices?
questions.explanation: He was Vice-President before becoming President.

questions.q: Narayanan's earlier diplomatic career placed him in which kind of service?
questions.explanation: His diplomatic career was in India's foreign service.

questions.q: The President whom Narayanan became is a constituent part of which legislative body?
questions.explanation: Parliament consists of the President and the two Houses.

questions.q: As Vice-President, Narayanan would act as President in which situation?
options: When the Rajya Sabha is dissolved
During a vacancy in the office of President
questions.explanation: The Vice-President acts as President during a vacancy in that office.

questions.q: The Vice-Presidency held by Narayanan is described as which office in constitutional rank?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President holds the second-highest constitutional office in India.

questions.q: Narayanan's term as President fell across which span of years?
questions.explanation: His 1997 to 2002 term spanned the late 1990s into the early 2000s.

questions.q: The Vice-President's role as Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, which Narayanan held earlier, is set out in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 64 makes the Vice-President the ex-officio Chairman of the Council of States.

questions.q: The office of Vice-President, held by Narayanan, is provided for under which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 63 provides that there shall be a Vice-President of India.

questions.q: The executive power of the Union, exercisable through the President whom Narayanan became, is vested by which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 53 vests the executive power of the Union in the President.

questions.q: The President, the office Narayanan held, acts on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 74 provides for a Council of Ministers to aid and advise the President.

questions.q: The Vice-President, the office Narayanan held, is elected by an electoral college consisting of whom?
options: Members of both Houses of Parliament
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is elected by the members of both Houses of Parliament.

questions.q: The office of President that Narayanan held is itself established by which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 52 states that there shall be a President of India.

questions.q: Which statement about the chronology of Narayanan's offices is accurate?
options: He served as Vice-President immediately before becoming President
He was President before serving as Vice-President
He held both offices at once
He never held the office of Vice-President
questions.explanation: He served as Vice-President directly before being elected President.

questions.q: The President's term of office, as held by Narayanan, runs for five years from which point under the Constitution?
options: The date the President enters upon the office
questions.explanation: The five-year term is counted from the date the President enters upon the office.
